channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: ADES to acquire Saipem’s Saudi rig-owning unit for $285m
item.description: ADES Holding, a Saudi Arabia-based oil and gas drilling services provider, has signed a deal worth $285m to acquire Saudi Arabian Saipem in a move to expand its offshore fleet.

item.title: Woodside Wins Contract to Supply Gas to Alcoa
item.description: Woodside executed an agreement with Alcoa to supply the alumina maker with 31.1 petajoules of Western Australian natural gas from 2027 to 2030.

item.title: Moscow Refinery May Stay Offline Until 2027
item.description: The Moscow Oil Refinery is highly unlikely to resume production before 2027 after suffering extensive structural damage from multiple strikes by Ukraine’s long-range drones, dealing a further blow to a country grappling…

item.title: Norway Well Workers Continue Strike as Standoff Escalates
item.description: The affected companies - including Baker Hughes, Halliburton and SLB - imposed a legal work halt to force the workers into agreeing to terms proposed under government mediation.

item.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Post Another Major Draw
item.description: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.1 million barrels during the week ending June 19, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease…

item.title: PLN EPI hot taps WNTS to link Conrad’s Mako gas project
item.description: Conrad Asia Energy has announced that PLN Energi Primer Indonesia (PLN EPI) has completed a "hot tap" connection to the West Natuna Transportation System (WNTS), facilitating a link to the Pemping gas pipeline project.

item.title: Baker Hughes wins service contract for Nigeria’s ANOH gas plant
item.description: Baker Hughes has been awarded a long-term service agreement by ANOH Gas Processing Company (AGPC) to support the operation of the ANOH Gas Processing Plant located in Nigeria.

item.title: UK Climate Panel Urges Faster Electrification to Lower Energy Bills
item.description: UK’s slower-than-expected electrification exposes households to fossil fuel price shocks, the country’s Climate Change Committee (CCC) said on Wednesday, urging faster electrification and removal of policy costs to…

item.title: Rubio Heads to Gulf as Iran Deal Sparks Anxiety Among U.S. Allies
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in Abu Dhabi on June 23, opening a high-stakes Gulf tour aimed at reassuring Washington’s closest Arab allies after a fragile cease-fire framework between the United States and…

item.title: Euro Sinks To One-Year Low As Oil Price Drop Fuels ECB Rate Cut Bets
item.description: The euro has sunk to a one-year low, with easing of tensions between the United States and Iran helping to cool oil prices and raise expectations that the European Central Bank will turn more dovish. The euro was quoted…

item.title: US, Qatar Push Back against EU Methane Rules
item.description: The countries asked for a 'pragmatic approach' to clarifying the rules and adopting changes to allow importers to continue to obtain oil and gas needed by the EU.

item.title: Asian Demand for Mideast Oil Slows after Splurge
item.description: Asian refiners have slowed purchases of Middle Eastern crude after a buying spree over the past three weeks, with oil majors and traders stepping in to take some of the surplus barrels.

item.title: Saipem begins offshore works for GranMorgu project in Suriname
item.description: Saipem has begun offshore operations for the GranMorgu project in Suriname following the arrival and mooring of the Normand Navigator construction vessel at Jules Sedney Harbour in Paramaribo.

item.title: VLCC Earnings Near $470,000 a Day as Hormuz Hopes Drive Tanker Frenzy
item.description: Oil tanker rates have soared since the U.S. and Iran announced the memorandum of understanding as oil importers scramble to charter vessels to pick up Persian Gulf cargoes in the hope these can transit the tentatively…

item.title: Adani Targets 10 GW Nuclear Power Capacity in India by 2035
item.description: Adani Group, the conglomerate of Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, could become India’s biggest private nuclear power capacity developer within a decade, targeting 10 gigawatts (GW) by 2035, as India opened its civil…

item.title: China’s Teapot Refineries Cut Operations to Their Lowest Level Since 2017
item.description: The independent refiners in China, the so-called teapots, have slashed their refinery run rates to the lowest level since 2017, as high feedstock prices, weak domestic fuel consumption, and restricted exports have hit…

item.title: India Cuts Coal Imports as Power Plants Turn to Domestic Fuel
item.description: India is boosting the share of domestic coal used in power plants designated to run on imported fuel, as rising local coal production and expanding renewables have helped to cut coal imports to multi-year lows. India…

item.title: Qatar Says LNG Exports Could Return to Normal Within Weeks
item.description: Qatar will be back to normal production of liquefied natural gas in a few weeks, the country’s prime minister, Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani, told the Financial Times today. “Within a few weeks, production…

item.title: Malaysian Major Makes Gas Discovery in Suriname
item.description: Malaysia’s state energy firm Petronas has made a natural gas discovery offshore Suriname, the country’s president said this week, as quoted by Reuters, expecting the Malaysian major to make the final investment decision…

item.title: Trump Orders Gas Price Gouging Probe
item.description: President Trump has ordered an investigation into possible price gouging at fuel stations, an investigation he announced in a social media post. “The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump…
